instructions:
Write Episode 2 ('Career') of the 4-episode 专题 series on Karla Silvestre (Montgomery County Council At-Large, Democratic, district MoCo countywide). Air date: 2026-05-31.

EPISODE 2 SCOPE — Career:
Professional history end-to-end, in chronological order, with employer + start/end years for every role. Public-facing work: media, op-eds, books, speeches, podcast interviews, X/Twitter and LinkedIn back to earliest archived posts. Court records, professional licensure, board memberships, charity work. Net worth and disclosed conflicts of interest.

MUST COVER (do not skip):
  • every job held with employer and dates
  • military service if any
  • boards / nonprofits / volunteer leadership
  • public statements outside politics (op-eds, books, podcast guest spots)
  • social media history — earliest posts you can find
  • court records (PACER + state) if any
  • publicly-disclosed financial picture

DOSSIER (READ FIRST): `C:\Users\evan_\local_politic_scan\data\candidate_dossiers\karla-silvestre.md` — this is the source of truth for biographical / career / political facts. Cite from it. If a section you need for episode 2 is missing or thin, STOP and write a fresh `candidate_dossier` brief into the inbox for this candidate before writing today's script.

FORMAT:
  • Two hosts ALEX (presenter) and JORDAN (curious co-host).
  • Every line starts `ALEX:` or `JORDAN:` — no stage directions,     no markdown, no emoji, no '[laughs]', no asterisks.
  • LENGTH TARGET — 3900 words (±300; ~30 min at 130 wpm).
  • Cite specific names, dates, employers, schools, bill numbers,     URLs from the dossier — generalities are why earlier drafts     felt thin.

PM LENGTH CALIBRATION:
  • No prior scripts to calibrate against. Aim for ~3900 words (~30 min).
  • Hit the target. If you finish the must-cover list early,     extend the JORDAN follow-up questions and add depth to the     biographical reads — never pad with filler.

AVOID — do not reuse any framing on `context.avoid_list`. The PM agent has flagged these as already-shipped this week:
  - 'Thirty-X days out from the Maryland Democratic primary' cold-open device — used 2026-05-16/17/18/19 ep1 and again 2026-05-25 Peter James ep1
  - 'Montgomery County native' framing hook — used heavily 2026-05-16 Friedson ep1
  - 'My Seven Black Fathers' / Reid Temple A.M.E. memoir-anchor for anyone other than Jawando — used 2026-05-19 ep1
  - 'Anniston, Alabama / Donoho School / Vaughn Stewart Jr as mayor' formative device for anyone other than Vaughn Stewart — used 2026-05-20 ep1
  - 'Born in Trenton / adopted as an infant from a teenage mom in a convent' origin device for anyone other than Dawn Luedtke — used 2026-05-17 ep1
  - 'First Muslim officer of the Montgomery County Police Department' identity device for anyone other than Sharif Hidayat — used 2026-05-18 ep1
  - 'Pre-political biography is thinner in the public record / no Wikipedia entry' candidate qualifier — used 2026-05-16 ep1
  - ICE Out Act / Trust Act / DHS officer screening bill as the immigration framing hook — used 2026-05-21 Glass ep3/ep4
  - 'Run the buses, teach the kids, pick up the trash' tagline — used 2026-05-21 Glass ep1/ep2/ep4
  - 'Welcome back ... It is Monday May twenty-fifth ... one month from tomorrow' calendar/countdown studio open — used 2026-05-25 Peter James ep1
  - Generic 'Welcome back to the Rockville Primary Briefing, I'm Alex' studio open — used 2026-05-27 Sayles ep1
  - 'Brass plaque on Maryland Avenue in Rockville listing the County Executives' cold-open — used 2026-05-29 Marc Elrich ep1
  - 'Day two / day three / day four of [Candidate] week' episode-open device — used heavily 2026-05-29 Marc Elrich set
  - Marc-Elrich-specific framings ('teacher and tenant organizer from Takoma Park', 'won by 32 votes', 'lighter than the council, louder than the council') — reserve for Elrich only, do not reuse for other candidates
  - RE-COVERING MARC ELRICH AT ALL — fully covered 2026-05-29 (4 valid episodes on disk). Gap-bug guard: do not let an unscheduled-gap day re-queue Elrich (it did on both 05-29 and 05-30).

LISTENER POSITIONING:
  • Locale: Rockville, Montgomery County, Maryland.
  • Their districts:
  - U.S. Congressional District: MD-8
  - MD Legislative District: 19
  - MD State Senate District: 19
  - MD House of Delegates District: 19
  - Senatorial District: 19
  - County Councilmanic District: 7
  - Election District: 8
  - Precinct: 08-008
  - Circuit Court District: 6
  - Appellate Circuit Court: 7
  - School District: 005
  - Party Central Committee: MCC19
  • The listener is a registered Democrat in Rockville, MD,     primary on June 23, 2026. Speak as though we already know     their districts; never tell them to 'look up their ballot'.
  • If any item from `context.listener_notes` ties into this     candidate, address it by name in the dialogue.

Write the full dialogue to `output_file`. Plain text only. If the dossier doesn't have the facts you need, do NOT invent — have a host say on-air 'we don't have a public record of X yet' and move on.
